summary: Section Editor UI testing completed. All 20 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified: tabs navigation, Add Section modal with 5 section types, section CRUD operations, visibility toggle, and public profile rendering. Fixed DELETE API to accept body for CDN compatibility.
passed_tests: Dashboard Profile Editor loads with 3 tabs: Profile Info, Link Blocks, Mini Website
Mini Website tab shows Section Editor with Add Section button
Add Section modal shows all 5 section types: About Me, Upcoming Event, Image Gallery, YouTube Video, Testimonials
Adding an About section opens Edit modal with Title, Description, Highlight Quote fields
Saving a section shows it in the sections list
Sections have visibility toggle (eye icon), edit button, delete button
Sections have drag handle for reordering
Visibility toggle changes icon from Eye to EyeOff and dims card
Public profile page renders enabled sections correctly
GET /api/sections - Returns sections array sorted by order
PUT /api/sections - Update section data and toggle visibility
DELETE /api/sections - Delete section (supports both query params and body)
POST /api/sections/reorder - Reorder sections and persist order
Authentication required for all section CRUD endpoints (401 without session)
seed_data_creation: Test sections created during testing were cleaned up. User 'testuser' has sections in the Mini Website tab.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Section Editor fully functional. 5 section types: about, retreat, gallery, video, testimonials. Sections stored in profiles collection under 'sections' array. Delete API supports both query params (internal) and body (CDN-friendly). Drag-and-drop reordering via GripVertical handle. Public profile only shows enabled sections sorted by order.
